\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:sheafTensorPow, def:sheafTensorObj,
    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
  We do \emph{not} appeal to a monoidal structure on the whole category
  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) --- none is available --- but only to the comparison
  on tensor \emph{powers} of the single invertible sheaf \(\mathcal{L}\). On such
  locally free factors the construction is elementary, and right-exactness of the
  tensor product is never invoked.

  \textbf{A local-iso criterion.} A morphism of sheaves of
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is an isomorphism as soon as it is one locally, i.e.\
  after restriction to each member of an open cover of \(X\): two-sided inverses
  defined over the members of a cover and agreeing on overlaps glue to a global
  inverse. We invoke this criterion \emph{only} on tensor products of sheaves
  that are locally free of rank one. On a trivialising open \(U\) where
  \(\mathcal{L}|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\), every tensor power restricts to
  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes k}|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\) as well; the tensor product
  presheaf there takes the value \(\mathcal{O}_X(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}
  \mathcal{O}_X(U) = \mathcal{O}_X(U)\); and the sheafification unit \(\eta\)
  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}) restricts over \(U\) to the
  identity. Hence over each such \(U\) the structural comparison maps used below
  --- the unitor, associator, and braiding of \cref{def:sheafTensorObj},
  descended objectwise from \cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib} --- are
  visibly bijective, and the criterion promotes them to genuine isomorphisms in
  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\). The subtlety that obstructs a general monoidal
  descent --- that the sheafification comparison
  \(\eta_{\mathcal P} \otimes \mathrm{id}\) need not be locally injective when
  \(\mathcal P\) is arbitrary, the tensor product being only right exact --- does
  not bite here: that object is never among the ones we test. The only maps we
  test are comparisons between locally free factors, where the unit is locally an
  identity and the comparison is a genuine local isomorphism.

  Concretely, each structural map is obtained as the image under the
  sheafification functor \(\mathrm{sheafification} : \PshMod(\mathcal{O}_X) \to
  \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}) of
  the corresponding natural transformation supplied by the presheaf-level
  monoidal structure \cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}: the associator
  \(\alpha\), the left/right unitors \(\lambda,\rho\), and the braiding
  \(\beta\) of \(\PshMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\). Sheafification is a functor, hence
  carries each such presheaf-level natural transformation to a morphism of
  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\); these are the maps the local-isomorphism criterion
  above promotes to isomorphisms on locally free factors.

  \textbf{Construction of \(\mu_{m,m'}\).} Fix \(m'\) and induct on \(m\). For
  \(m = 0\),
  \[
    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes 0} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
      = \mathcal{O}_X \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
      \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
  \]
  is the left unitor of \cref{def:sheafTensorObj}, an isomorphism by the
  criterion. For the inductive step the recursive definition
  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)} = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}\)
  (\cref{def:sheafTensorPow}) gives
  \[
    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
    = (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}) \otimes
      \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes
      (\mathcal{L} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'})
    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes
      (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'} \otimes \mathcal{L}),
  \]
  using first the associator and then the braiding of \cref{def:sheafTensorObj},
  each an isomorphism by the criterion. A second associator and the inductive
  comparison \(\mu_{m,m'}\) whiskered by \(\mathcal{L}\) then identify the
  right-hand side with
  \[
    (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}) \otimes
      \mathcal{L}
    \xrightarrow{\ \mu_{m,m'} \otimes \mathrm{id}\ }
    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m + m')} \otimes \mathcal{L}
    = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m + m' + 1)},
  \]
  the final equality being once more the recursive definition. The composite is
  \(\mu_{m+1,m'}\), an isomorphism as a composite of isomorphisms. Naturality in
  \(\mathcal{L}\) holds because each constituent comparison is natural.

  \textbf{Commutativity and associativity constraints.} Every map appearing in
  the construction is a structural comparison isomorphism between locally free
  factors, so the required coherences are equalities of morphisms that may be
  checked locally on a common trivialising cover. Over such an open every sheaf
  in sight becomes \(\mathcal{O}_U\) and every comparison map becomes the
  identity, whence both sides of the commutativity square (\(\mu_{m,m'}\) versus
  \(\mu_{m',m}\) postcomposed with the braiding) and of the associativity
  diagram (the two bracketings of
  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'} \otimes
  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m''} \to \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+m'+m'')}\)) reduce to the
  same local identity. Gluing the local equalities yields the global cocycle
  relations: the family \(\{\mu_{m,m'}\}\) is coherent in the
  pentagon/hexagon sense.

  \textbf{Remark on the principled route.} One could instead obtain \(\mu\) from
  a genuine monoidal structure on \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) descended from the
  presheaf-level monoidal structure (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib})
  along sheafification, via the localized-monoidal / reflective-localization
  template for sheaf categories. That principled construction requires a
  monoidal-closed structure on \(\PshMod\) that is not built in this chapter, and
  is deferred. The bespoke line-bundle construction above is all the section
  graded ring requires.
\end{proof}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:sheafTensorPow, def:sheafTensorObj, cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc,
    lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit,
    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
  We do \emph{not} build a full monoidal structure on the whole category
  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) --- none is constructed here --- but only assemble
  the comparison \(\mu_{m,m'}\) from those structural isomorphisms of
  \cref{def:sheafTensorObj} that the induction actually uses: the associator,
  the braiding, and the left unitor.

  \textbf{The structural maps.} The braiding and the unitors descend to
  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) directly: each is the sheafification of the
  corresponding isomorphism of the presheaf symmetric monoidal structure
  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), and sheafification, being a
  functor, carries isomorphisms to isomorphisms. The \emph{associator} is not
  the sheafification of a single presheaf isomorphism, because the iterated
  sheaf tensor products
  \((\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B}) \otimes \mathcal{C}\) and
  \(\mathcal{A} \otimes (\mathcal{B} \otimes \mathcal{C})\) each carry an inner
  sheafification (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}); it is supplied instead by
  \cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}, whose construction clears those inner
  sheafifications using the strong-monoidality comparison
  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}. These are the only
  isomorphisms of sheaves of modules the construction invokes; right-exactness
  of the tensor product is never used.

  \textbf{Construction of \(\mu_{m,m'}\).} Fix \(m'\) and induct on \(m\). For
  \(m = 0\),
  \[
    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes 0} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
      = \mathcal{O}_X \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
      \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
  \]
  is the left unitor of \cref{def:sheafTensorObj}. For the inductive step the
  recursive definition
  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)} = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}\)
  (\cref{def:sheafTensorPow}) gives
  \[
    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
    = (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}) \otimes
      \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
    \xrightarrow{\ \alpha\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes
      (\mathcal{L} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'})
    \xrightarrow{\ \mathrm{id} \otimes \beta\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes
      (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'} \otimes \mathcal{L}),
  \]
  using the associator \(\alpha\) (\cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}) and then the
  braiding \(\beta\) of \cref{def:sheafTensorObj} whiskered into the left
  factor. The inverse associator \(\alpha^{-1}\)
  (\cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}) and the inductive comparison \(\mu_{m,m'}\)
  whiskered by \(\mathcal{L}\) on the right then carry this to
  \[
    (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}) \otimes
      \mathcal{L}
    \xrightarrow{\ \mu_{m,m'} \rhd \mathcal{L}\ }
    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m + m')} \otimes \mathcal{L}
    = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes ((m + m') + 1)},
  \]
  the final equality being the recursive definition. The reindexing
  \((m + m') + 1 = (m + 1) + m'\) identifies the target with
  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes ((m+1) + m')}\), and the composite is
  \(\mu_{m+1,m'}\), an isomorphism as a composite of isomorphisms. Naturality in
  \(\mathcal{L}\) holds because each constituent comparison is natural.

  \textbf{Commutativity and associativity constraints.} The braiding and
  unitors are the sheafifications of the symmetric-monoidal coherence data of
  \(\PshMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), and
  the associator is intertwined with the presheaf associator by the comparison
  isomorphisms of \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit} through the
  construction of \cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}. Both the commutativity square
  (\(\mu_{m,m'}\) versus \(\mu_{m',m}\) postcomposed with the braiding) and the
  associativity pentagon (the two bracketings of
  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'} \otimes
  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m''} \to \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+m'+m'')}\)) are therefore
  images, under the sheafification functor together with these comparison
  isomorphisms, of the corresponding coherence diagrams of the presheaf
  symmetric monoidal structure, which commute by Mac\,Lane coherence. Since a
  functor preserves commuting diagrams and the comparison isomorphisms are
  natural, the constraints hold for the family \(\{\mu_{m,m'}\}\). We emphasise
  that this argument uses only the single strong-monoidality comparison
  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit} and the resulting associator
  \cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}, not a full monoidal structure on
  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
\end{proof}
